item.title: 5/17: Sunday Morning "By Design"
item.description: Jane Pauley hosts our annual look at design. Featured: Tiny homes in your backyard; Louis Vuitton; Adobe house construction; Finland's Marimekko; domino art; Philadelphia food favorites; chandeliers; Longwood Gardens; rare maps; and director Jon Favreau of "The Mandalorian and Grogu."

item.title: LIRR strike has many worrying about Monday's commute. Here's the latest.
item.description: While the LIRR work stoppage has caused a mess for people heading to weekend events​, the real concern is Monday morning's commute.

item.title: Full transcript of "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 17, 2026
item.description: On this "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" broadcast, U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer and Ambassador Alexander Yui, Taiwan's Representative to the U.S., join Margaret Brennan.

item.title: "Call Her Daddy" podcast host Alex Cooper announces pregnancy
item.description: "Call Her Daddy" host Alex Cooper said on Instagram that she and husband Matt Kaplan are expecting their first child.

item.title: Video shows rescuers treating mountain climber who fell 50 feet
item.description: Rescuers airlifted an injured hiker who fell about 50 feet from the summit of Washington state's Mount Si on Saturday.

item.title: Hamas leader who helped plan Oct. 7 attacks has been killed, Israel says
item.description: Izz al-Din al-Haddad was killed in a "precise strike in the area on the City of Gaza," the Israel Defense Forces said Saturday.

item.title: Robert Gates says "it would be a mistake" for the U.S. to change its rhetoric on Taiwan
item.description: After President Trump said he and Chinese President Xi Jinping "talked a lot about Taiwan," former Defense Secretary Robert Gates told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that he believes "it would be a mistake to change the carefully worded position of the United States with respect to Taiwan."

item.title: Transcript: Reps. Brian Fitzpatrick and Tom Suozzi on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 17, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with Reps. Brian Fitzpatrick, Republican of Pennsylvania, and Tom Suozzi, Democrat of New York, that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on May 17, 2026.

item.title: Transcript: Alexander Yui, Taiwan's representative to the U.S., on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 17, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with Ambassador Alexander Yui, Taiwan's representative to the U.S., that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on May 17, 2026.

item.title: Transcript: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 17, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on May 17, 2026.

item.title: Senate GOP revising White House ballroom security funds plan
item.description: The Senate's rulemaker delivered a blow to GOP plans to fund security for President Trump's overhaul of the East Wing of the White House.

item.title: Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick says gerrymandering is "corrosive" to "our democracy"
item.description: Republican Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick joined Democratic Rep. Tom Suozzi, the co-chairs of the House Problem Solvers Caucus, on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," saying that gerrymandering is "one of the most, if not the most, corrosive things to our democracy that I can imagine."

item.title: Trump says "Clock is Ticking" for Iran as shaky ceasefire continues
item.description: President Trump said "there won't be anything left of them" if Iranian leaders do not "get moving, FAST."

item.title: Ronda Rousey defeats Gina Carano seconds into joint comeback match
item.description: Ronda Rousey used her signature armbar to stop Gina Carano just 17 seconds into a comeback bout between the two MMA fighters.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (May 17): "By Design"
item.description: "Sunday Morning" presents its annual edition touching on all aspects of design, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Taiwan representative to U.S.: "We're not the ones creating all this trouble"
item.description: Taiwan's representative to the U.S. Alexander Yui said on Sunday that "we want peace and stability" as Taiwan became among the most closely-watched issues in last week's summit.

item.title: Letlow, Fleming advance to runoff in Louisiana GOP Senate primary, CBS News projects
item.description: Incumbent GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy, who had occasionally broken with the Trump administration, lost his bid for a third term.

item.title: Taiwan's representative to U.S. says "we're not the ones creating all this trouble" with China
item.description: As Chinese state media reported that President Xi Jinping told President Trump there would be "conflicts" if the situation with Taiwan isn't "handled properly," Alexander Yui, Taiwan's representative to the U.S., told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that Taiwan is "not the ones creating all this trouble" in the region.

item.title: 5/17: Face The Nation
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," as President Trump returns from his trip to China, U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer and Ambassador Alexander Yui, Taiwan's representative to the U.S., join. Plus, Anthony Salvanto has the latest CBS News polling on the economy.

item.title: Jamieson Greer says China-U.S. Board of Trade will be "focused on trade in non-sensitive goods"
item.description: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that the newly-announced Board of Trade between the U.S. and China will be focused on "non-sensitive goods" such as "sales of agricultural goods to China, energy goods, Boeings, medical devices."

item.title: Open: This is "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 17, 2026
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," as President Trump returns from his trip to China, U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer and Ambassador Alexander Yui, Taiwan's representative to the U.S., join. Plus, Anthony Salvanto has the latest CBS News polling on the economy.

item.title: Irish researchers find oldest English poem in medieval book in Rome
item.description: Researchers in Dublin discovered the oldest surviving English poem in a Roman library.

item.title: Nature: Chanticleer Garden
item.description: We leave you this Sunday at Chanticleer Garden just outside Philadelphia. Videographer: Jim Zurich.

item.title: Understanding the world through old maps
item.description: The Osher Map Library, at the University of Southern Maine in Portland, is home to half a million rare maps, globes and atlases, dating as far back as the 15th century. Correspondent Martha Teichner explores the world as depicted by cartographers, through maps that are whimsical, political, or intentionally distorted, and examines why – in an age of GPS – maps are definitely not outdated.

item.title: Extended interview: Jon Favreau
item.description: In this web exclusive, Jon Favreau, the creator of the "Star Wars" series "The Mandalorian," and director of the new film "The Mandalorian and Grogu," talks with correspondent Tracy Smith about the myth of "Star Wars," the influences on the story (including Japanese samurai films), and expanding the universe for a new audience. He also talks about working with Robert Downey Jr. on his film "Iron Man," and about cooking, the subject of his 2014 movie, "Chef."

item.title: Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda declared global health emergency
item.description: At least 80 deaths have been reported in a new Ebola disease outbreak in Congo and Uganda, authorities said.

item.title: CBS News poll finds many voice frustration with Trump's economic approach
item.description: Gas prices, inflation weigh on Americans. Many see lack of clarity on what's happening in Iran.

item.title: Exorbitant World Cup ticket prices creating sticker shock for soccer fans
item.description: FIFA President Gianni Infantino joked that if someone does pay $2 million for a ticket to the World Cup final, "I will personally bring him a hot dog and a Coke."

item.title: Ice cream sold in 17 states recalled for potential metal fragments
item.description: No injuries have been reported because of the issue, the Food and Drug Administration said.

item.title: Disney to close its "Star Wars" hotel that opened last year
item.description: The final guest stay will be September 30, so anyone who booked a reservation for beyond that date should contact Disney.
